top_signals.title: Stocks Fall, Dollar Climbs as Focus Turns to Warsh: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Equities started July on a cautious note as investors awaited comments from Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh at a time when traders are ramping up bets on US interest rate hikes. The dollar rose.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Lost in Translation: How A Premier Chinese Think Tank Views U.S.-Chinese Competition
top_signals.summary:
On May 13, 2026, Air Force One landed in Beijing for President Donald Trump’s first state visit to China in nearly a decade. That same morning, the China Institutes of Contemporary International Relations published a report titled The Evolving World and the Right Way to China-US Coexistence. The summit dominated global media coverage for two days. The report received almost none.The Beijing summit produced familiar imagery: honor guards, a state banquet in the Great Hall of the People, and carefully choreographed warmth. top_signals.title: ‘Unprecedented’ plane crash tests Beijing’s sense of security
top_signals.summary: Fatal flight of a light aircraft into Chinese capital’s tallest building raises questions about its defences
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, May 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income increased $181.6 billion (0.7 percent at a monthly rate) in May, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-increased $164.9 billion (0.7 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $156.1 billion (0.7 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $159.9 billion in May. Personal saving was $704.2 billion in May, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 3.0 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Circle shares sink 16% after Open USD reveal, analysts say fears are ‘overblown’
top_signals.summary: William Blair reiterated its Outperform rating on Circle's CRCL stock, calling Tuesday's selloff a buying opportunity.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: GDP, (Third Estimate), Industries, Corporate Profits, State GDP, and State Personal Income, 1st Quarter 2026
top_signals.summary: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 2.1 percent in the first quarter of 2026 (January, February, and March), according to the third estimate released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. In the fourth quarter of 2025, real GDP increased 0.5 percent. The contributors to the increase in real GDP in the first quarter were increases in investment, exports, government spending, and consumer spending. Imports, which are a subtraction in the calculation of GDP, increased. At the state level, real GDP ranged from a 4.5 percent increase in Washington to a 1.6 percent decrease in South Dakota. Full Text

Chubu Electric continued to misrepresent quake risk at nuclear plant amid probe
top_signals.summary: The power supplier allegedly underestimated the seismic ground motion the plant could experience, using a calculation method different from that reported to the NRA.
